title: Next.js middleware Location header reused as routing URL regardless of status code
description: When middleware returns a response carrying a Location header, getResolveRoutes converts the value with getRelativeURL and assigns it to parsedUrl, treating it as a redirect target. The fix gates this conversion on a real redirect status (3xx). Without the gate, custom middleware that reflects request headers (or any attacker-influenced value) into a non-redirect Location header causes the router to follow the supplied URL on the server side, producing SSRF in self-hosted Next.js applications.
